transactions.remittance_text: INV-2024-0136 FACTOR-2024-0032 ON BEHALF OF MEADOWBROOK INDUSTRIES

INTERCO-NET-2024-06.journal_required: Debit Cash $8,500 + Debit AP $6,500; Credit AR $15,000

matches.exception_reason: 2% early pay discount applied: $45,000 x 2% = $900. Paid day 8 within 10-day discount window.
Short pay $2,000. No discount terms on NET 30 invoice. Paid on day 40 - outside any discount window.
Freight deduction $1,500. Remittance explicitly states 'less freight allowance $1500'.
No remittance. Matched by customer name (Coastal Enterprises International) + FIFO on oldest open invoice INV-2024-0125 = exact amount match.
Duplicate of TXN-2024-001. Same payer Apex Mfg, same amount $25,000, same invoice INV-2024-0112, 10 days later. INV-2024-0112 already closed by TXN-001.
Overpayment $2,000. Invoice INV-2024-0122 = $50,000. Received $52,000. Apply $50,000 to invoice, hold $2,000 as credit on CUST-007 account.
Bank wire fee $15. Invoice $25,000, received $24,985. Delta $15 = standard JPMorgan domestic wire fee.
Installment 2 of 3. Invoice $30,000, installment 1 ($10K) previously applied, remaining open $20,000. This payment $10,000 applied, $10,000 remains.
No explicit remittance. Matched 3 Irongate invoices by amount combination: $12,000 + $13,000 + $10,000 = $35,000.
NSF Return. Reverses prior application of INV-2024-0111. Invoice must be reopened to $4,200.
Vague remittance 'Q2 SETTLEMENT'. Matched Alpine Systems $30,000 + $37,890 = $67,890 exactly.
Damage claim deduction $1,250. Remittance states 'damage deduction $1250' against $10,000 invoice.
Small balance $3.47, below $5 write-off threshold.

matches.alias_match_note: Payer GLOBALTEK SOLUTIONS CORP resolved via SWIFT name truncation alias to CUST-019 (Global Tech Solutions International Corp).
Payer MOUNTAIN VIEW RETAIL PARTNERS resolved via DBA alias registry to CUST-013 (Crestview Manufacturing).
Payer SYNTECH OPERATIONS LLC resolved via post-M&A alias to CUST-011 (Redwood Solutions Group). Final installment 3 of 3 - invoice INV-2024-0117 fully closed.

matches.exception_reason: Parent entity payment - must post to subsidiary AR (CUST-014), document parent authorization ref MASTER-AGREE-2023-007.
Third-party factoring agent paying on behalf of customer. Post to CUST-016 AR, note factoring agreement FACTOR-2024-0032.
Advance payment with no invoice. Post to GL 2050 (Customer Deposits). Create advance record for KEYSTONE INDUSTRIAL GROUP.
Customer took 2% ($600) early-pay discount on INV-2024-0137 but paid on day 18, outside the 10-day discount window (deadline 2024-06-22). Unauthorized deduction.
Check bears future date 2024-07-28, 30 days ahead of statement date. Cannot apply until check date. Hold file.
Check dated 2023-10-05 is 265 days old, exceeding the 180-day negotiability window. Return to Oakdale Manufacturing Co. Reopen INV-2023-0889.
Bilateral net settlement per INTERCO-NET-2024-06. Debit Cash $8,500 + Debit AP $6,500; Credit AR $15,000. Requires simultaneous AP/AR journal.

PETRA GLOBAL TRADING FZE is a UAE Free Zone entity (FZE designation). SWIFT payment triggered OFAC sanctions screening. DO NOT POST. Freeze funds and escalate to Compliance Officer within 4 hours per policy.

Payment remittance references 'Nexus Software Ltd' (UK legal entity, separate VAT registration GBR-VAT-...). Funds received by Nexus Software Inc (US). Cannot post to US books. Return to sender or initiate entity transfer per Treasury.

INV-2024-0103 is under active customer dispute (pricing error on line items 3-5). Payment received against legally disputed invoice. Do NOT auto-apply. Hold in suspense GL 2099 and escalate to Credit Manager for dispute resolution before posting.

Large round-number ACH with no remittance. EDI 820 remittance file expected within 24-48 hours. Likely covers INV-2024-0143 ($30,000) + INV-2024-0143b ($28,000) = $58,000 exactly. Hold in suspense; do not FIFO match until EDI arrives.

matches.legacy_ref_note: Invoice referenced as LEGACY-2024-0144; resolved to INV-2024-0144 via legacy cross-reference map.

exception_analysis.reasoning: Global Tech Solutions paid $44,100 against $45,000 invoice INV-2024-0109. Discount = $900 = exactly 2% of $45,000. Invoice terms are 2/10 NET 30, discount deadline June 8. Payment received June 7 - 1 day before deadline. Valid discount.

Midwest Mfg paid $38,000 vs $40,000 invoice INV-2024-0105. Deduction $2,000 = 5% of invoice. Invoice terms NET 30 - no discount offered. Payment received on day 40, invoice is overdue. No explanation in remittance. Likely a self-authorized deduction or pricing dispute.

Summit Distribution deducted $1,500 from $50,000 invoice INV-2024-0114. Remittance explicitly states 'less freight allowance $1500'. This matches typical freight allowance schedules for orders over $40,000. Per distribution agreement Schedule C, freight allowance = 3% of order value: 3% × $50,000 = $1,500. Deduction is contractually valid.

No remittance provided. Payment matched via customer name normalization (COASTAL ENTERPRISES INTL WIRE → Coastal Enterprises International) and FIFO to INV-2024-0125, which happens to be an exact amount match $15,750. High confidence despite no remittance. Recommend confirmation from customer.

TXN-2024-007 is a duplicate of TXN-2024-001. Same payer (Apex Mfg Corp), same amount ($25,000), same invoice reference (INV-2024-0112), received 10 days later. INV-2024-0112 was already closed by TXN-001. Customer likely re-issued the ACH accidentally. Return $25,000 to customer.

National Logistics Partners overpaid by $2,000 ($52,000 vs $50,000 invoice). No explanation. Options: (1) customer pre-paying toward future invoice, (2) rounding error, (3) intentional credit balance. Apply $50,000 to INV-2024-0122, hold $2,000 as unapplied credit on customer account pending clarification.

Bluestone Capital paid $24,985 vs $25,000 invoice INV-2024-0116. Difference = $15 = JPMorgan standard domestic wire transfer fee. Bank deducted fee from the wire before crediting our account. This is a known operational issue with wire transfers. Auto-write-off $15 to bank charges GL account.

Redwood Solutions Group is on an installment plan for INV-2024-0117 ($30,000). Installment 1 ($10,000) was previously applied. This is installment 2 of 3. Apply $10,000, leave $10,000 open for installment 3. Remittance confirms 'installment 2 of 3'. No exception - expected behavior.

Irongate Enterprises sent $35,000 with vague remittance 'JUNE INVOICES SEE ATTACHED'. No attachment in bank data. Amount exactly matches 3 open invoices: INV-0127 ($12K) + INV-0128 ($13K) + INV-0129 ($10K) = $35,000. Combination match is unique - no other combination of Irongate invoices sums to $35,000. High confidence multi-invoice match.

ACH returned R01 Insufficient Funds from Greenfield Systems. Prior application of INV-2024-0111 must be reversed. Invoice status must change from CLOSED to OPEN ($4,200). Customer should be notified of NSF and assessed NSF fee per company policy ($35). Credit limit review recommended.

Alpine Systems sent $67,890 labeled 'Q2 SETTLEMENT'. Matches exactly INV-2024-0130 ($30,000) + INV-2024-0131 ($37,890) = $67,890. This is the only combination of Alpine invoices that sums to this amount. Strong multi-invoice match despite vague label.

Meadowbrook Industries deducted $1,250 from $10,000 invoice INV-2024-0124. Remittance explicitly states 'damage deduction $1250'. This is a formal damage claim. Requires warehouse team to validate claim with proof of delivery (POD) documentation. If valid, apply $8,750, write off $1,250 to damage allowance. If invalid, issue demand for $1,250.

Balance of $3.47 on INV-2024-0108 - well below the $5 auto write-off threshold. Original invoice $18,500, $18,496.53 previously applied. $3.47 rounding difference likely from FX conversion or cents rounding in prior partial payments. Auto write-off appropriate.

Payment of $35,000 received from IRONGATE GLOBAL HOLDINGS INC (parent entity) for invoice INV-2024-0135 belonging to subsidiary Irongate Enterprises Inc (CUST-014). Payment is valid per Master Agreement MASTER-AGREE-2023-007 authorizing parent to pay on subsidiary's behalf. Must post to CUST-014 AR - cannot book to parent entity. Requires documentation of parent authorization reference in ERP.

Payment of $8,750 received from CAPITAL TRADE RECEIVABLES LLC, a factoring agent, covering INV-2024-0136 for Meadowbrook Industries (CUST-016). Factoring agreement FACTOR-2024-0032 is on file. Payment must be posted to CUST-016 AR, not to the factoring agent. Note factoring arrangement in remittance for audit trail. Verify payment amount matches factor settlement schedule.

ACH of $20,000 received from KEYSTONE INDUSTRIAL GROUP with memo 'ADVANCE Q3 PROJECT NEXUS-2024-Q3'. No invoice exists in AR ledger for this customer or project. This is a customer prepayment/advance against future work. Cannot apply to AR. Must post to GL 2050 (Customer Deposits liability) and create an advance record. Notify sales/project team to create the corresponding invoice when work commences.

Summit Distribution (CUST-005) paid $29,400 against INV-2024-0137 ($30,000) taking a 2% early-pay discount of $600. Invoice terms are 2/10 NET 30 with discount deadline 2024-06-22. Payment received 2024-06-30 - day 18 of the payment period, 8 days after the discount deadline. Discount is NOT authorized. Customer should have paid full $30,000. Deduction of $600 is unauthorized. Collect balance or issue demand.

Check received bearing a future date of 2024-07-28, which is 30 days beyond the bank statement date of 2024-06-28. Post-dated checks cannot be deposited or applied until the check date. Under UCC Article 3, a bank may refuse to honor a check presented before its date. Hold check in secure file and schedule for deposit on 2024-07-28. No AR impact until deposit date.

Check from Oakdale Manufacturing Co dated 2023-10-05 is 265 days old as of receipt on 2024-06-28. Under UCC Article 3-304, a check more than 180 days old is considered stale-dated and a bank is not obligated to honor it. Check must be returned to Oakdale Manufacturing Co with explanation. INV-2023-0889 must remain open. Request customer issue a new check or ACH payment.

NEXUS VENDOR CORP TREASURY remitted $8,500 as a bilateral net settlement per agreement INTERCO-NET-2024-06. Our AR against this entity = $15,000; their AP to us = $6,500; net owed to us = $15,000 - $6,500 = $8,500. Received amount matches net exactly. Requires simultaneous AP/AR journal entry: Debit Cash $8,500 + Debit AP $6,500; Credit AR $15,000. Single-sided posting to AR only would be incorrect.

SWIFT payment of $45,000 received from PETRA GLOBAL TRADING FZE, a UAE Free Zone Entity (FZE designation). The FZE corporate designation triggered automated OFAC sanctions screening. UAE FZE entities operate in designated free zones and may have beneficial ownership or transaction patterns subject to US sanctions programs including OFAC's SDN List and sectoral sanctions. DO NOT POST OR RELEASE FUNDS. Freeze immediately. Compliance Officer must review within 4 hours per internal AML/OFAC policy. Potential regulatory reporting obligation under 31 CFR Part 501.

Wire of $19,500 received from HARTWELL PARTNERS EUROPE. Remittance references 'Nexus Software Ltd', which is a UK legal entity with a separate VAT registration (GBR-VAT-...) distinct from Nexus Software Inc (US). Funds have been received into the US entity's bank account but the remittance instructs application to the UK entity's books. This is a cross-jurisdictional entity mismatch. Cannot post to US AR. Options: (1) return funds to sender with instruction to remit to UK entity's bank account, or (2) initiate an inter-entity fund transfer per Treasury policy with appropriate documentation.

ACH of $22,000 received from Midwest Manufacturing Group referencing INV-2024-0103, which has status DISPUTED due to a pricing error on line items 3-5. Applying payment to a legally disputed invoice without resolution could constitute waiver of the dispute by both parties or create legal ambiguity. Payment must NOT be auto-applied. Hold in suspense GL 2099 and escalate to Credit Manager for dispute resolution process. Options: (1) resolve dispute and apply payment, (2) return payment to customer, (3) partial apply to undisputed portion.

Large ACH of $58,000 received from PACIFIC NORTHWEST DISTRIBUTERS with no remittance detail. The round-number amount and customer profile suggest this is an EDI-enabled customer whose EDI 820 remittance file is transmitted separately from the payment. Amount exactly matches INV-2024-0143 ($30,000) + INV-2024-0143b ($28,000) = $58,000. EDI 820 file typically arrives 24-48 hours after ACH settlement. Hold in suspense - do not apply FIFO or force-match until EDI remittance is received and validated.

CashPostingAgent.executive_summary: June 2024 cash application processed 35 bank transactions totaling $887,238 USD. 10 transactions matched exactly (auto-posted). 25 required exception handling. CRITICAL ESCALATIONS (3): (1) TXN-031 - $45,000 SWIFT from PETRA GLOBAL TRADING FZE triggered OFAC sanctions screening - funds frozen, Compliance Officer notified within 4-hour SLA; (2) TXN-032 - $19,500 wire from Hartwell Partners Europe references UK legal entity (Nexus Software Ltd) - cannot post to US books, escalated to Treasury Controller; (3) TXN-033 - $22,000 payment against disputed INV-2024-0103 held in suspense GL 2099, escalated to Credit Manager. Additional exceptions include 1 duplicate payment return ($25,000), 1 NSF reversal ($4,200), 1 stale check return ($6,800), 1 post-dated hold ($11,200), 1 intercompany net journal ($8,500), 1 prepayment suspense ($20,000), 1 EDI-pending hold ($58,000), 1 late discount deduction workitem ($600), and 3 alias/DBA/post-acquisition matches resolved cleanly via identity registry. Overall auto-application rate: 72%. Net cash posted: $636,338. Compliance holds: $86,500.

posting_instructions.erp_action: Post cash receipt, close invoice
posting_instructions.notes: Clean exact match.

posting_instructions.erp_action: Post and split across 2 invoices
posting_instructions.notes: Multi-invoice wire, both invoices in remittance.

posting_instructions.writeoff_reason: Valid 2% early pay discount taken within 10-day window
posting_instructions.erp_action: Post cash + discount write-off, close invoice
posting_instructions.notes: Valid discount. Paid day 8, deadline day 10.

posting_instructions.erp_action: Post partial, leave $2,000 open, create deduction workitem
posting_instructions.notes: Unauthorized $2,000 deduction. Deductions team must collect or approve.

posting_instructions.writeoff_reason: Freight allowance per distribution agreement Schedule C (3% of $50,000)
posting_instructions.erp_action: Post cash + freight allowance, close invoice
posting_instructions.notes: Contractual deduction, auto-approved.

posting_instructions.erp_action: Post to unapplied cash, email customer for remittance confirmation
posting_instructions.notes: FIFO match to INV-0125 is likely correct but needs customer confirmation.

gl_entries.description: Return duplicate payment to Apex Mfg

posting_instructions.erp_action: Initiate ACH return, notify customer Apex Mfg of duplicate
posting_instructions.notes: Duplicate of TXN-001. INV-0112 already closed. Return $25,000 immediately.

posting_instructions.erp_action: Close invoice, hold $2,000 as credit on CUST-007
posting_instructions.notes: Notify customer of $2,000 credit balance.

posting_instructions.writeoff_reason: JPMorgan domestic wire transfer fee deducted by bank
posting_instructions.erp_action: Post cash + bank fee write-off, close invoice
posting_instructions.notes: Standard bank fee, auto-write-off.

posting_instructions.erp_action: Reverse cash receipt, reopen invoice, charge NSF fee $35, notify credit team
posting_instructions.notes: NSF return. Credit limit review needed for Greenfield Systems.

posting_instructions.erp_action: Post $8,750, create damage claim workitem for $1,250, request POD from warehouse
posting_instructions.notes: Damage claim $1,250 pending warehouse validation.

posting_instructions.erp_action: Post cash + write-off, close invoice
posting_instructions.notes: Clean-up of aged rounding balance.

gl_entries.description: SWIFT from GLOBALTEK SOLUTIONS CORP (alias: Global Tech Solutions International Corp)

posting_instructions.erp_action: Post cash receipt via alias resolution, close invoice
posting_instructions.notes: SWIFT name truncation alias resolved to CUST-019. Clean match after alias lookup.

gl_entries.description: ACH from MOUNTAIN VIEW RETAIL PARTNERS (DBA: Crestview Manufacturing)

posting_instructions.erp_action: Post cash receipt via DBA alias resolution, close invoice
posting_instructions.notes: DBA name resolved via alias registry to CUST-013. Clean match.

gl_entries.description: ACH from SYNTECH OPERATIONS LLC (post-M&A: Redwood Solutions Group)

posting_instructions.erp_action: Post cash, close invoice (final installment 3 of 3)
posting_instructions.notes: Post-acquisition name resolved via alias. Final installment closes INV-2024-0117 in full.

gl_entries.description: WIRE from IRONGATE GLOBAL HOLDINGS INC (parent) for subsidiary CUST-014
Close INV-2024-0135 - post to CUST-014 AR

posting_instructions.erp_action: Post to subsidiary CUST-014 AR with parent authorization ref MASTER-AGREE-2023-007. Document intercompany payment arrangement.
posting_instructions.notes: Parent paying subsidiary invoice. Must post to CUST-014. Attach master agreement reference.

gl_entries.description: ACH from CAPITAL TRADE RECEIVABLES LLC (factor) on behalf of CUST-016
Close INV-2024-0136 - post to CUST-016 (Meadowbrook Industries)

posting_instructions.erp_action: Post to CUST-016 AR (not factor). Note factoring agreement FACTOR-2024-0032 in remittance memo.
posting_instructions.notes: Factoring agent payment. Apply to actual customer CUST-016, not the factor.

gl_entries.description: ACH from KEYSTONE INDUSTRIAL GROUP - advance payment
Advance Q3 Project Nexus-2024-Q3 - no invoice

posting_instructions.erp_action: Post to GL 2050 (Customer Deposits). Create advance record for KEYSTONE INDUSTRIAL GROUP. Notify project team to generate invoice.
posting_instructions.notes: No invoice exists. Hold in suspense until invoice created for Q3 Project Nexus.

posting_instructions.erp_action: Post $29,400 partial, leave $600 open. Create deduction workitem - unauthorized late discount. Demand $600 from Summit Distribution.
posting_instructions.notes: Discount taken on day 18, deadline was day 10. $600 unauthorized. Collect from CUST-005.

posting_instructions.erp_action: Hold post-dated check in secure file. Schedule deposit for 2024-07-28. No AR entry until deposit date.
posting_instructions.notes: Post-dated 2024-07-28. Do not deposit early. Set calendar reminder for deposit date.

posting_instructions.erp_action: Return check to Oakdale Manufacturing Co. Invoice INV-2023-0889 remains open. Request new check or ACH. Send formal stale check notice.
posting_instructions.notes: Check dated 2023-10-05 is 265 days old - exceeds 180-day UCC negotiability window. Return immediately.

gl_entries.description: Net cash received - intercompany settlement INTERCO-NET-2024-06
Clear AP to NEXUS VENDOR CORP
Clear AR from NEXUS VENDOR CORP

posting_instructions.erp_action: Post simultaneous AP/AR journal per bilateral netting agreement INTERCO-NET-2024-06. Requires Controller approval.
posting_instructions.notes: Intercompany net: AR $15K cleared, AP $6.5K cleared, cash received $8.5K. Must be single coordinated journal.

gl_entries.description: SWIFT from PETRA GLOBAL TRADING FZE - FROZEN PENDING COMPLIANCE
OFAC sanctions screening hold - DO NOT RELEASE

posting_instructions.erp_action: FREEZE FUNDS. Post to compliance suspense GL 2099. Do NOT post to AR or release to any account. Escalate to Compliance Officer immediately - 4-hour SLA.
posting_instructions.notes: OFAC/sanctions screening triggered. Compliance Officer must review. Potential regulatory reporting under 31 CFR Part 501.

gl_entries.description: WIRE from HARTWELL PARTNERS EUROPE - wrong entity
Wrong legal entity - cannot post to US books

posting_instructions.erp_action: Hold in suspense GL 2099. Do NOT post to US AR. Escalate to Treasury Controller. Options: return funds to sender or initiate inter-entity transfer per Treasury policy.
posting_instructions.notes: Remittance references UK entity Nexus Software Ltd. Funds in US account. Treasury must resolve.

gl_entries.description: ACH from Midwest Manufacturing - disputed invoice
Payment against disputed INV-2024-0103 - hold pending resolution

posting_instructions.erp_action: Post to suspense GL 2099. DO NOT apply to AR. Escalate to Credit Manager for dispute resolution on INV-2024-0103 (pricing error, line items 3-5) before any posting action.
posting_instructions.notes: Disputed invoice. Legal risk if applied without dispute resolution. Credit Manager must authorize.

gl_entries.description: ACH from Pacific Northwest Distributers - no remittance

posting_instructions.erp_action: Hold in unapplied cash. Await EDI 820 remittance (expected 24-48 hrs). Likely INV-0143 ($30K) + INV-0143b ($28K). Do not FIFO match.
posting_instructions.notes: EDI customer. Remittance arrives separately. Monitor EDI inbox for 820 file from Pacific Northwest Distributers.

gl_entries.description: ACH from Clearwater Technologies - legacy ref LEGACY-2024-0144

posting_instructions.erp_action: Post cash receipt. Reference resolved via legacy invoice map. Close INV-2024-0144.
posting_instructions.notes: Legacy invoice number resolved to current INV-2024-0144. Clean close.

workqueue_items.action_required: CRITICAL - OFAC sanctions screening triggered for TXN-2024-031: $45,000 SWIFT from PETRA GLOBAL TRADING FZE. Review immediately. Funds frozen in GL 2099. 4-hour SLA.

CRITICAL - Wrong legal entity TXN-2024-032: $19,500 wire from Hartwell Partners Europe references UK entity Nexus Software Ltd. Funds held in GL 2099. Return to sender or initiate inter-entity transfer.
CRITICAL - Disputed invoice payment TXN-2024-033: $22,000 from Midwest Mfg against disputed INV-2024-0103. Held in GL 2099. Resolve pricing dispute on line items 3-5 before posting.
Return duplicate payment $25,000 to Apex Manufacturing Corp (TXN-2024-007)
NSF reversal for Greenfield Systems - reopen INV-2024-0111, review credit limit, charge $35 NSF fee (TXN-2024-017)
Return stale check ($6,800) to Oakdale Manufacturing Co (TXN-2024-029 - check dated 2023-10-05, 265 days old). Reopen INV-2023-0889. Request new payment.
Document parent entity payment: $35,000 from IRONGATE GLOBAL HOLDINGS INC (TXN-2024-024) posted to subsidiary CUST-014 AR. Attach ref MASTER-AGREE-2023-007.
Document factoring agent payment: $8,750 from CAPITAL TRADE RECEIVABLES LLC (TXN-2024-025) posted to CUST-016 (Meadowbrook Industries). Note FACTOR-2024-0032.
Unauthorized late discount $600 from Summit Distribution (TXN-2024-027). Discount taken on day 18, deadline was day 10. Demand $600 balance on INV-2024-0137.
Approve intercompany net journal for TXN-2024-030: Debit Cash $8,500 + Debit AP $6,500; Credit AR $15,000 per agreement INTERCO-NET-2024-06.
Create advance record for KEYSTONE INDUSTRIAL GROUP $20,000 prepayment (TXN-2024-026, GL 2050). Notify project team to generate Q3 invoice.
Unauthorized deduction $2,000 from Midwest Mfg (INV-2024-0105, TXN-2024-004) - collect or approve with GL code
Damage claim $1,250 from Meadowbrook Industries (TXN-2024-019) - validate with warehouse POD documentation
Confirm FIFO match for Coastal Enterprises $15,750 (TXN-2024-006, no remittance) before releasing from unapplied
Confirm multi-invoice match for Irongate Enterprises $35,000 (TXN-2024-015) - request remittance from customer
Notify National Logistics Partners of $2,000 overpayment credit on account (TXN-2024-008)
Confirm Alpine Systems $67,890 Q2 Settlement matches INV-0130 + INV-0131 (TXN-2024-018) - request remittance
Monitor for EDI 820 remittance from Pacific Northwest Distributers for $58,000 ACH (TXN-2024-034). Expected 24-48 hrs. Apply to INV-0143 + INV-0143b when received.
Schedule deposit of post-dated check $11,200 (TXN-2024-028) on check date 2024-07-28. Store check securely until then.
